expectations.text: Flags that state must be rechecked after releasing and reacquiring the trace lock
expectations.evidence: Blocking #1 explicitly flags the race in the partial flush path: 'After the partial flush path releases t.mu.Unlock() at line 706, it later re-acquires t.mu.RLock() at line 715 to call setTraceTagsLocked(fSpan). Between the unlock and re-lock, another goroutine could modify t.tags or t.propagatingTags.' It notes that values captured before unlock could be stale.

expectations.text: Flags that trace-level tags are iterated without holding the trace lock
expectations.evidence: Blocking #1 describes the concern that setTraceTagsLocked reads t.tags and t.propagatingTags after a window where the trace lock was released and re-acquired, meaning the state could have changed between the unlock and the RLock. The review identifies this as 'a subtle but real concern in the partial flush path.'
